lessons.title: 1.2.2 WRITTEN LITERATURE
topics: Written Literature has a number of genres that appeal to the mind of the audience. One such
genre is drama. Drama is a genre of literature and performance that involves a serious emotional,
or thought-provoking narrative, presented in front of the audience.
activities: performed on stage: plot, character (actor), dialogue, setting, conflict, staging, theme,
symbolism, irony
 Introduce drama and its elements to the learners and how drama can be performed as
provided in the excerpt below.
“The Price of a Shortcut”
(Stage Director- provides stage directions in this play)
Characters (actors)
Mwamba – A Form 1 learner, smart but tempted by shortcuts.
assessment: Assign topics to learners on which they can write scripts to be acted in the next lesson
Conclude the lesson by highlighting the following;
 Drama is a genre of literature that is intended to be performed by actors in front of an
audience. It can also be experienced through reading scripts.
 Plot- the sequence of events in a play.
 Characters (Actors)-performers of actions in a play.
